steps.title: Identify the angle relationship at the intersection
steps.content:
When two lines intersect, adjacent angles (angles sharing a common side at the vertex) form a linear pair. These angles are supplementary, meaning they sum to \$180°$.

From the figure, $w°$ and $z°$ are adjacent angles at the intersection point.

steps.title: Apply the linear pair property and solve for z
steps.content: Since $w$ and $z$ form a linear pair, they satisfy: $$w + z = 180°$$ Substitute $w = 128°$: $$128 + z = 180°$$ $$z = 52°$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
